inlineContent.text: Materials define the appearance of 3D models in RealityKit, by defining how their surfaces interact with light. Materials can also occlude objects behind them, hold shaders, or act as portals to view other worlds with
RealityKit includes a few material types to help you get started, including
for basic color and textures, and
for realistic lighting effects such as reflections, roughness, and emission.

abstract.text: Apply textures to the surface of your scene’s 3D objects to give each object a unique appearance.
An alias for the texture coordinate transform that’s appropriate for this material class.
An invisible material that hides objects rendered behind it.
Create personalized and realistic spatial audio experiences.
Errors generated when loading custom material functions.
An object that defines the transparency options for a custom material.
An object that you use to configure new
objects.
Display a horizontal row of three-dimensional shapes in your visionOS app, using predefined mesh and white material.
Build a RealityKit component to support standard visionOS gestures on any entity.
A container for resources that will be encoded into a CustomMaterial’s custom uniforms argument buffer.
An object that specifies all parameters necessary to initialize
programs
An alias for the texture coordinate transform that’s appropriate for
this material class.
An object that defines an entity’s exposure to ambient light.

abstract.text: Bring an iOS or iPadOS game to visionOS and enhance it.
